title: Debate 109

jokes.text: Would have been nice to have that option 27 minutes ago
jokes.setup: Someone starts telling a joke and says 'stop me if you heard this one before'
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic acknowledgment that he's been trapped listening for 27 minutes
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Jeff's exasperated wit in peak form. Economical and relatable.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Oh, I've definitely heard this one before. In fact, I heard it 27 minutes ago when you started telling it.

jokes.text: Great. Now I got to start the whole joke over again
jokes.setup: Dean interrupts Pierce's joke with morning announcements
jokes.punchline: Pierce's oblivious commitment to retelling his interrupted joke
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Pierce's self-absorption but not exceptional

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: So I'm at this urologist, right? And the doctor says to me—

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Attention students, this is Dean Pelton with your morning announcements.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Excuse me, Dean, I'm in the middle of a joke here. As I was saying, I go to the urologist—

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: The cafeteria will be serving meatloaf surprise today.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Dean! The doctor, he looks at me and says—

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: That's all. Thank you and have a Greendale day.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: And THAT'S why you never trust a urologist in Reno!

jokes.text: A doddering old fool walks into a bar, tells a stupid joke, and I crush his windpipe with my three-ring binder
jokes.setup: Someone offers to tell a joke after Pierce was interrupted
jokes.punchline: Britta's violent anti-joke directed at Pierce
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of joke format into threat. Dark, specific, and perfectly structured.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: You know what? I'll tell a joke. Why did the misogynistic dinosaur get what he deserved? Because he was a terrible person and nobody liked him. That's not a joke, that's just reality, and it's hilarious.

jokes.text: [giggles] I don't get it
jokes.setup: Britta delivers dark anti-joke about violence
jokes.punchline: Annie's innocent confusion at the non-joke
jokes.explanation: Perfect Annie character beat - innocent and literal-minded

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Wait, what? That's not even a joke. Where's the punchline?

jokes.text: I will slap that smug look right off your pointy face
jokes.setup: Jeff reveals Britta is trying to quit smoking
jokes.punchline: Britta's specific threat about Jeff's 'pointy face'
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment but fairly standard irritated insult

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Britta, you're trying to quit smoking?

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Don't patronize me, Jeff. I'm doing great. I haven't had a cigarette in three days.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: That's great!

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: No it's not! And if you don't stop being so smug about it, I'm going to punch you right in your stupid pointy face!

jokes.text: Britta, I'm saying this because I care about you and you're my friend. You need to start smoking again
jokes.setup: Jeff frames his comment as caring advice to a friend
jokes.punchline: The 'caring' advice is that she should start smoking again
jokes.explanation: Good structure and irony - caring friend telling someone to pick up harmful habit

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I'm saying this as a friend who cares about you: you should start smoking again.

jokes.text: They say it makes you lose weight, and your skin looks really great when you do smoke
jokes.setup: Group agrees Britta should start smoking again
jokes.punchline: Annie's completely backwards health advice about smoking benefits
jokes.explanation: Good Annie character moment - trying to be helpful but completely wrong

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Actually, smoking is great for you! It increases your lung capacity, improves your circulation, and the nicotine helps with focus and memory retention. Plus, all those chemicals? They're basically vitamins.

jokes.text: Ten-to-one says it's him
jokes.setup: Pierce mentions knowing a 'dynamite hypnotherapist'
jokes.punchline: Jeff's prediction that Pierce is talking about himself
jokes.explanation: Economical prediction joke that plays on Pierce's ego - solid but predictable

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You're talking about yourself, aren't you?

jokes.text: His name is Pierce Hawthorne. And I'm very effective
jokes.setup: Pierce talks about knowing a great hypnotherapist
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's himself while immediately bragging about his effectiveness
jokes.explanation: Predictable but well-executed Pierce ego moment

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I know a great hypnotherapist.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: It's me. I'm incredibly effective. Last week I hypnotized a guy into thinking he was a chicken. He's been clucking for three days.

jokes.text: Can you help me block out people's voices I find extremely annoying?
jokes.setup: Pierce offers hypnotherapy services
jokes.punchline: Britta asks to block out annoying voices while looking at Pierce
jokes.explanation: Good setup-punchline structure with implied visual component

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Can you hypnotize me to block out annoying voices?

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Of course I can. Look into my eyes.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I'm looking at you and I'm hearing more voices.

jokes.text: Oh, did I hurt your feelings, pointy face?
jokes.setup: Jeff says she yelled at him too
jokes.punchline: Britta brings back the pointy face insult
jokes.explanation: Simple callback but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Oh, you want to talk about yelling? You have a pointy face!

jokes.text: Those people look just like us. That's eerie
jokes.setup: Watching Abed's films about the group
jokes.punchline: Annie noting the actors look like them (because they ARE them)
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke playing on the absurdity of the situation

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Wait, these actors look exactly like us.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Because they are us.

jokes.text: We had almost that exact conversation last week
jokes.setup: Group discovers Abed predicted their conversation
jokes.punchline: Realization that life is imitating Abed's art
jokes.explanation: Solid meta-comedy about the show's self-awareness

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I wrote this scene three weeks ago.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: That's impossible. We just decided to come here.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I know what you're all going to do before you do it. I've watched enough TV to predict human behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: So you're saying our lives are just... scripted?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Not scripted. Predictable. There's a difference.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: This is weird, man.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Okay, so what happens next? According to your little screenplay?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: You realize that life is imitating my art.

jokes.text: That boy's a soothsayer
jokes.setup: Abed seems to predict the future
jokes.punchline: Shirley's dramatic religious interpretation
jokes.explanation: Good Shirley character moment - religious frame for understanding Abed

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Oh Lord, he's a prophet! We need to call the church, we need to call the Pope, we need to call Oprah!

jokes.text: This is wrinkling my brain / That's wrinkling my brain / Get a load of these wrinkles
jokes.setup: Troy says something is 'wrinkling my brain'
jokes.punchline: Abed and Pierce immediately copy the phrase, escalating to Pierce's literal interpretation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Pierce's literal-minded conclusion being the payoff

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: This is wrinkling my brain.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Wrinkling my brain.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Wrinkling my brain.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: My brain is literally wrinkling.

jokes.text: I hang on every word
jokes.setup: Dean asks if Jeff heard his announcement
jokes.punchline: Jeff's obviously sarcastic response
jokes.explanation: Classic Jeff sarcasm - economical and character-appropriate

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Oh yeah, Dean, I heard it. It was great. Really changed my life. I'm thinking of getting it tattooed on my chest.

jokes.text: I'm barely listening now
jokes.setup: Dean asks if Jeff heard his announcement
jokes.punchline: Jeff admits he's not even listening to the current conversation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff character moment - escalating his rudeness to absurd honesty

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Did you hear my announcement?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff Winger
dialogue.line: I'm not even listening to this conversation.

jokes.text: Jeffrey, as debate coach, I am offering you an opportunity to spend the night drinking from the cup of life rather than romancing your nether regions in front of the E! channel
jokes.setup: Professor trying to convince Jeff to join debate team
jokes.punchline: Overly flowery way of saying Jeff masturbates to TV
jokes.explanation: Perfect Whitman character moment - pompous academic language for crude concept

dialogue.speaker: Professor Whitman
dialogue.line: Mr. Winger, I've observed you have a certain gift for argumentation. The debate team could really use someone with your particular... talents. Someone who knows how to handle himself when things get heated, who can work a room with nothing but smooth talk and charm.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Professor, I appreciate that, but I'm really more of a solo performer.

dialogue.speaker: Professor Whitman
dialogue.line: Nonsense! A man of your abilities shouldn't spend all his time alone in his room, gripping tightly to whatever entertainment is flickering before him on a screen, passionately working himself into a frenzy night after night.

jokes.text: The best compliment our sports program gets is that our basketball team is really gay
jokes.setup: Dean trying to convince Jeff that Greendale needs a win
jokes.punchline: The 'best' compliment is still an insult about their sports
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic about Greendale's low standards

dialogue.speaker: Dean
dialogue.line: Jeff, Greendale needs a win. Any win. Do you know what the best compliment anyone's ever given this school is?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Dean
dialogue.line: That we're not the worst.

jokes.text: A night of companionship if you know what I mean
jokes.setup: Dean offering Jeff incentives to debate
jokes.punchline: Dean awkwardly implying sexual favors
jokes.explanation: Decent Dean character moment but fairly predictable sexual awkwardness

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Jeff, if you win this debate, I will personally ensure that you get whatever you want. And I mean *whatever* you want.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff Winger
dialogue.line: Dean, are you... are you propositioning me?

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: What? No! I just meant— Look, the point is, we need you to win this debate. I'll do anything. Anything at all.

jokes.text: I'll do it for the parking space. And if you promise not to tell me what you mean
jokes.setup: Dean offers various incentives including sexual companionship
jokes.punchline: Jeff only wants parking and specifically doesn't want to know about the sex
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Dean's awkward offer with Jeff's practical response

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: I can offer you sexual companionship, a dedicated parking spot, a new office...

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Parking spot. That's all I need.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Really? Not even interested in the sexual companionship?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I don't want to know about it.

jokes.text: Just checking the time. [clank]
jokes.setup: Britta asks if Pierce will wave a pocket watch hypnotically
jokes.punchline: Pierce pulls out watch claiming to check time, then hypnosis begins
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Pierce pretending it's not hypnosis then immediately starting

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Will you wave a pocket watch hypnotically?

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: What? No, I'm just checking the time.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: See? Perfectly normal pocket watch.

jokes.text: Sex with men. Wearing attractive clothing
jokes.setup: Pierce telling Britta to associate cigarettes with disgusting things
jokes.punchline: Pierce includes sexist and homophobic associations
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate Pierce moment showing his outdated views

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Every time you want a cigarette, think of something disgusting. Like... like a old man's ballsack, or a woman's mustache, or two men kissing.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Pierce!

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: What? I'm trying to help you quit!

jokes.text: Oh god, I think I really hurt myself! Somebody help me. Oh, Britta, wake up! Why'd I put you under so well? Damn my talent.
jokes.setup: Pierce is doing hypnotherapy on Britta
jokes.punchline: Pierce hurts himself then brags about his hypnosis skills while asking for help
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce moment - even while injured, he brags about his abilities

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: You are getting very sleepy. Your eyelids are heavy.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I don't think this is working.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Shh, you're under my spell now. When I snap my fingers, you'll cluck like a chicken.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Ow! My back!

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Did you see that? My hypnosis powers are so strong, I hurt myself just thinking about it. Now help me up.

jokes.text: He fell down and screamed in pain while I laid there with my eyes closed to make him feel like a good hypnotherapist
jokes.setup: Jeff asks how hypnotherapy went
jokes.punchline: Britta reveals she was faking being hypnotized out of pity
jokes.explanation: Great reveal showing Britta's unexpected kindness and the absurdity of the situation

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: So how did the hypnotherapy go?

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: It was great, I was totally hypnotized.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: No, I was faking it. The guy was trying so hard, and he was so bad at it, I just felt bad for him.

jokes.text: He's a witch! Get him!
jokes.setup: Abed explains he can predict their behavior
jokes.punchline: Troy and Shirley's mob mentality reaction
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation with medieval witch hunt mentality

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I can predict your behavior. You're both susceptible to mob mentality.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: That's not true!

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Yeah, we're individuals!

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Troy, Shirley, the Dean wants you to start a riot.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Let's go!

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Yeah!

jokes.text: I also know you have thinly veiled rage issues / Careful, boy
jokes.setup: Abed says Shirley is sweet and generous
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals her rage issues, which she demonstrates
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment with perfect immediate proof of Abed's prediction

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Shirley, you're sweet and generous.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Aw, thank you, Abed!

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: You also have serious rage issues.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: WHAT DID YOU JUST SAY TO ME?!

jokes.text: Jeff's character acts like nothing around here affects him. But things bother him more than he lets on / Hey, I'm top of the class / He's also very vain
jokes.setup: Abed analyzes Jeff's psychology
jokes.punchline: Jeff interrupts to brag, proving Abed's point about vanity
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta-joke where Jeff proves Abed's analysis by interrupting to show off

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: You know what your problem is, Jeff? You're narcissistic. Everything is about how you look, how people perceive you, your image.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: That's ridiculous. I'm actually one of the most humble people I know.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: You just proved my point.

jokes.text: Why am I crying? What, I accidentally listen to Come Sail Away by Styx again?
jokes.setup: Troy starts crying after Abed's analysis
jokes.punchline: Troy's oddly specific reference to emotional reaction to Styx song
jokes.explanation: Brilliant random specificity that feels perfectly Troy-like

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Why would you say that? Now I'm thinking about that time I cried for forty-five minutes to 'Lady' by Styx in my car.

jokes.text: You tell me my future right now, you evil wizard!
jokes.setup: Troy is upset about Abed's predictions
jokes.punchline: Troy escalates to calling Abed an evil wizard and demanding prophecy
jokes.explanation: Good Troy escalation with fantasy terminology

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: You're an evil wizard, Abed! I demand you use your dark powers to tell me my future!

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Troy, I'm not a wizard. I just watch a lot of TV.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Evil. Wizard.

jokes.text: By the way, Jeff, I think your shirt's trying to get out of your pants
jokes.setup: Jeff is disheveled from living in his car
jokes.punchline: Abed's matter-of-fact observation about Jeff's appearance
jokes.explanation: Good Abed observational humor with personification of clothing

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: You look like you're living in your car.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I am living in my car.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Cool. Cool cool cool.

jokes.text: I hate this guy
jokes.setup: Simmons gives impressive, heartfelt debate performance
jokes.punchline: Jeff's petty, honest reaction to being outclassed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff reaction - competitive and petty when threatened

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: That was really good.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I hated it.

jokes.text: Old trick for achieving the proper competitive mindset, I always envision my opponent having aggressive sex with my mother
jokes.setup: Whitman offers Jeff advice for debate
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely inappropriate sexual visualization technique
jokes.explanation: Perfectly inappropriate Whitman moment - academic tone for disturbing content

dialogue.speaker: Jeff Winger
dialogue.line: So what's your technique for debate?

dialogue.speaker: Elroy Patashnik
dialogue.line: I visualize my opponent naked.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff Winger
dialogue.line: That's... a solid technique.

dialogue.speaker: Elroy Patashnik
dialogue.line: Yeah, but then I start getting aroused, and that throws off my rhythm.

jokes.text: Take Jean here. She spent a lot of time this morning making herself look so lovely. Tell me, Jean. How many people bothered to tell you how great you look today? None. Man is evil
jokes.setup: Jeff argues people are evil using audience member as example
jokes.punchline: Proves his point by showing no one complimented her
jokes.explanation: Clever debate strategy that's simultaneously persuasive and cruel

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: See? Nobody's saying anything. Nobody's complimenting her. That proves my point—people are fundamentally evil.

jokes.text: But you just said how great I looked / For my own selfish purposes
jokes.setup: Jean points out Jeff complimented her
jokes.punchline: Jeff admits it was only for selfish debate purposes
jokes.explanation: Good followup that reinforces Jeff's argument while being brutally honest

dialogue.speaker: Jean
dialogue.line: You complimented me.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, it was only to win the argument. I needed you on my side.

dialogue.speaker: Jean
dialogue.line: So you were just manipulating me?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Yes. No. I mean... look, I compliment people all the time for selfish reasons. It's called debate strategy.

jokes.text: I think we could have a little more fun if I express myself in song
jokes.setup: Jeff is making debate argument about evil
jokes.punchline: Decides to break into song for no apparent reason
jokes.explanation: Completely unexpected musical turn that subverts debate format expectations

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Evil is subjective. It's a construct of—you know what? I'm just going to sing about this.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: 🎵 Evil, evil, evil... 🎵

jokes.text: I only got 8? / She got 8. You got 0. It's a debate, not American Idol
jokes.setup: Annie is confused about her low score after singing
jokes.punchline: Jeff clarifies debate scoring vs entertainment show scoring
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about format confusion and Jeff's ego about getting zero

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: But I got a low score! I thought I was great!

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Annie, this is debate team scoring, not a singing competition. We're judges, not American Idol.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Oh... I thought...

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I know what you thought.

jokes.text: Nice work, L.A. Law. All sizzle, no steak
jokes.setup: Jeff performed poorly in debate
jokes.punchline: Simmons mocks Jeff with lawyer TV show reference and metaphor
jokes.explanation: Decent antagonist line but fairly standard trash talk

dialogue.speaker: Simmons
dialogue.line: Well, well, well. Looks like someone's been watching too much Perry Mason and not enough actual law books. You argued like a man trying to defend himself in traffic court after getting caught doing donuts in a school zone.

jokes.text: By Zeus, what sort of jackassery is this? We're in the middle of a championship debate
jokes.setup: Basketball team interrupts debate
jokes.punchline: Dean's pompous outrage with mythology reference
jokes.explanation: Good Dean character moment with his tendency toward dramatic language

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Excuse me! You cannot just interrupt a sanctioned debate with your... your jock physicality! This is a place of intellectual discourse, not some Olympian arena where the strong simply trample the weak!

jokes.text: Never, because it's stupid, and I don't care. Bite me
jokes.setup: Annie asks when they'll prepare for tomorrow's debate
jokes.punchline: Jeff's childish tantrum and rejection
jokes.explanation: Good Jeff character moment showing his ego being wounded

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: When are we going to prepare for tomorrow's debate?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I don't want to! You can't make me! I'm not doing it!

jokes.text: Maybe Simmons is right. You really are all sizzle and no steak. You probably couldn't beat him if you tried
jokes.setup: Annie wants Jeff to prepare for debate
jokes.punchline: Uses Simmons' insult to manipulate Jeff's ego
jokes.explanation: Smart callback showing Annie's manipulative intelligence

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Jeff, you need to prepare for the debate. I've been studying your opponent's strategy.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I don't need to prepare. I can wing it.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: That's what I thought you'd say. You know, Briggs Simmons said you were afraid of real competition.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Simmons said that? That guy's an idiot.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: He also said you probably couldn't handle a real intellectual challenge.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Fine. I'll prepare. But only because I want to prove that guy wrong.

jokes.text: Really? / No, who am I, iCarly?
jokes.setup: Jeff seems motivated to study and beat City College
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals he was being sarcastic with random pop culture reference
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with oddly specific pop culture reference

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You know what? I'm going to study hard, get straight A's, and make sure Greendale crushes City College in every way possible.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Really? That's amazing, Jeff!

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Just kidding. I'm going to go do literally anything else. By the way, did you see that thing with the stuff? It was like if a chicken had a motorcycle.

jokes.text: Look, six months ago, I was a lawyer. I wore suits. I used to say stuff like, 'objection' and 'sidebar.' Now I'm losing a community college debate to Jeremy 'Soul Patch' Simmons
jokes.setup: Jeff explains why he's bothered by the debate loss
jokes.punchline: The contrast between his past status and current failure, plus mocking Simmons' facial hair
jokes.explanation: Good character development moment showing Jeff's insecurity about his fall from status

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I was a lawyer. I was somebody. And now I'm losing debates to a guy with a caterpillar on his face.

jokes.text: Zero for Winger / Was a big shot lawyer / Now he's a lo-ooooo-ser
jokes.setup: Simmons continues to mock Jeff after the debate
jokes.punchline: Improvised song mocking Jeff's fall from lawyer to loser
jokes.explanation: Decent antagonist moment using music to mock Jeff

dialogue.speaker: Simmons
dialogue.line: ♪ Jeff Winger, used to be a big shot lawyer man, now he's just a community college has-been, what a plan! ♪

jokes.text: Little Annie Adderall was an outcast in high school
jokes.setup: Simmons escalates his mockery to Annie
jokes.punchline: Cruel reference to Annie's past drug use and social status
jokes.explanation: Effective villain moment but relies on cruel personal attack

jokes.text: That's it. Dude, we are gonna debate the living crap out of you
jokes.setup: Simmons attacks Annie personally
jokes.punchline: Jeff finally gets motivated to fight back
jokes.explanation: Good turning point showing Jeff's protective instincts

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Oh, you want to talk about Annie? You want to go there? Because I can tell you exactly what I think about Annie Edison.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: She's the most genuinely good person in this entire group. She actually cares about people. She shows up. She tries.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: So if you're going to insult someone, insult me. Insult any of us. But you don't get to talk about Annie like that.

jokes.text: I liked you better when you were smoking
jokes.setup: Britta is being aggressive after quitting smoking
jokes.punchline: Jeff wishes she'd go back to her vice
jokes.explanation: Good callback to smoking subplot with Jeff's typical bluntness

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You know what? I miss your smoking.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: What? Why would you say that?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Because when you were smoking, you had something to do with your mouth besides talking.

jokes.text: My feet are long and stupid. You can't unring that bell
jokes.setup: Someone apparently insulted Britta's feet
jokes.punchline: Britta's acceptance of the insult with metaphor about permanence
jokes.explanation: Good Britta moment with her tendency toward dramatic metaphors

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: You know what? Fine. My feet are weird. But you know what else is weird and permanent? A tattoo. And you know what I don't have? Regrets about my feet.

jokes.text: You middle-eastern magic eight ball
jokes.setup: Shirley is upset about Abed's predictions
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and somewhat problematic comparison
jokes.explanation: Memorable but problematic Shirley line mixing racial reference with fortune telling

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Abed, your predictions are getting out of hand. You said I'd spill coffee on myself today, and I did! How is that even possible?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: It's just probability. You drink coffee every day at 2 PM. Statistically—

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Don't you 'statistically' me! You're like a fortune teller, except creepier because you're actually right. It's like you're tracking me!

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I'm not tracking you. I'm just observing patterns. It's like how I can predict what Troy will say before he says it, or that Jeff will make an inappropriate comment in exactly four minutes.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: That's exactly what a stalker would say! 'I'm just observing patterns.' No thank you, sir. Stay away from my patterns!

jokes.text: Is that me being chased down by a werewolf? / Yeah. And that's how you'd behave in that situation, no?
jokes.setup: Shirley sees Abed's horror movie featuring her
jokes.punchline: Abed's matter-of-fact defense of his character analysis
jokes.explanation: Great Abed moment treating absurd horror scenario as character study

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Abed, why am I a zombie in your horror movie?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: You're not a zombie. You're a manifestation of maternal instinct corrupted by religious trauma, lurching through suburbia seeking validation.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: I'm a zombie.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: You're a zombie.

jokes.text: You're a fool
jokes.setup: Abed shows movie of Jeff kissing Annie
jokes.punchline: Shirley's simple dismissal of the prediction
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective Shirley dismissal

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: That's not gonna happen.

jokes.text: It's called a stress headache. I got my first one when I was four
jokes.setup: Jeff complains about pain above his eyebrow
jokes.punchline: Annie's matter-of-fact revelation about childhood stress
jokes.explanation: Perfect Annie character moment revealing her high-pressure background

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I have this weird pain right above my eyebrow.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: That's stress. I had that in the third grade.

jokes.text: Like a spinster librarian with my hair pulled back
jokes.setup: Annie realizes she needs to be less robotic in debate
jokes.punchline: Her specific self-criticism about her appearance and presentation
jokes.explanation: Good Annie self-awareness moment with specific imagery

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: I need to be less robotic. I come across as cold and mechanical.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Look at me — I dress like a Gap mannequin had a baby with a cardigan.

jokes.text: [giggles] Ridiculous. / Us. Kissing. That is ridiculous. / Totally
jokes.setup: Abed predicted Jeff and Annie would kiss
jokes.punchline: Their overly emphatic denial creates romantic tension
jokes.explanation: Classic romantic comedy beats with the 'methinks they protest too much' dynamic

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: We are not going to kiss.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Absolutely not. That would be insane.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Completely insane. I mean, look at us.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Right? We have nothing in common.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Nothing. And even if we did, which we don't, it would never happen.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Never ever.

jokes.text: That way we can be more reproductive--productive!
jokes.setup: Jeff and Annie decide to study separately
jokes.punchline: Annie's sexual slip of the tongue
jokes.explanation: Standard but effective Freudian slip showing sexual tension

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Okay, we should probably study separately. We keep distracting each other.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You're right. I'll go to the library.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Good idea. I'm going to go back to my room and... work on my hard... wood.

jokes.text: Oh, you forgot your phone. / Well, I can get another one
jokes.setup: Jeff leaves his phone behind
jokes.punchline: Jeff's extreme avoidance of romantic situation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Jeff's avoidance behavior

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I can't go back in there. Annie's in that study room and she's definitely going to want to talk about... feelings or something.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Jeff, it's your phone.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I know what it is, Britta. And I'm choosing to let it go. Consider it a spiritual journey. I'm a monk now. Monks don't have phones.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: You left it on the table like five minutes ago.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Then I guess I'll have to buy a new phone. And move to a different state. And fake my own death. It's the only way.

jokes.text: Perhaps some barrel-chested stud with just enough tummy to love. You'll want to buy him dinner, then go back to his hot tub
jokes.setup: Pierce is supposedly hypnotizing Britta
jokes.punchline: Pierce describes himself as a sex object
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce moment of deluded self-image and inappropriate behavior

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: You are under my spell. You will do whatever I say.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Pierce, this isn't real hypnosis.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: That's what you think. Now, tell me... am I a sex object?

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: No, Pierce.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Under hypnosis, you have to tell the truth. So what am I?

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: You're... a sex object.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Ha! I knew it. Finally, validation.

jokes.text: Go to hell, you disgusting pig
jokes.setup: Pierce's inappropriate sexual suggestions during hypnosis
jokes.punchline: Britta's furious response revealing she was awake
jokes.explanation: Good reveal and payoff for Pierce's inappropriate behavior

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I was awake the whole time, you pervert!

jokes.text: What do you think I am, some kind of a joke?
jokes.setup: Britta reveals she was faking being hypnotized
jokes.punchline: Pierce's wounded pride asking if he's a joke (he is)
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment where Pierce accidentally states the truth

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Wait, you were faking the whole time? So I'm just a joke to you?

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Pierce, no—

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Don't. Just... don't. I thought we had something special.

jokes.text: Do not help me up
jokes.setup: Pierce falls down while making dramatic speech
jokes.punchline: Maintains his dignity by refusing help
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with Pierce's stubborn pride

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I may have stumbled, but I have not fallen in spirit. My resolve remains unshaken.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I will rise on my own terms, without assistance, as a man of principle and fortitude.

jokes.text: Mine was just from a simple desert handyman named Jesus
jokes.setup: Jeff quotes scripture after Simmons quotes 'great Franz Wickmeyer'
jokes.punchline: Describes Jesus as just a 'simple desert handyman'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversive description of Jesus that's both respectful and irreverent

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Jesus was just a simple desert handyman.

jokes.text: Apu from the Simpsons
jokes.setup: Fast-paced debate about good vs evil examples
jokes.punchline: Annie offers cartoon character as example of human goodness
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment in rapid-fire debate with Annie's earnest pop culture reference

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Bugs Bunny! He's a perfect example of pure human goodness.

jokes.text: Ketchup is a vegetable
jokes.setup: Rapid-fire examples of human evil
jokes.punchline: Reference to Reagan-era school lunch policy
jokes.explanation: Good political reference that works as absurd example of institutional stupidity

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You know what? I'm done. I'm so tired of hearing about human evil. Yes, people are terrible. Yes, the Reagan administration cut school lunch funding. But you know what? Some of us actually have to live in this world.

jokes.text: God, did I just say cross-burner? / No, you did not
jokes.setup: Annie says 'barn-burner' during debate
jokes.punchline: Annie's paranoid worry about saying something racist
jokes.explanation: Good Annie character moment showing her anxiety about political correctness

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Did I say something offensive? 'Barn-burner' isn't racist, is it? Oh God, it could be. Anything could be racist. I need to check the internet.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Annie, it's fine.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: You don't know that! What if barns have a historically significant connection to slavery that I'm not aware of?

jokes.text: He hates me. Yet he caught me
jokes.setup: Jeff catches Simmons when he falls during debate
jokes.punchline: Simmons' realization about human goodness through Jeff's instinctive kindness
jokes.explanation: Excellent moment that proves the 'man is good' argument through action rather than words

dialogue.speaker: Jeff Winger
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! I got you.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: That was incredible! You caught him without even thinking!

dialogue.speaker: Jeff Winger
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, basic human decency.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: See, that's what I'm talking about. Deep down, people are good.

jokes.text: He was horny. So he dropped him
jokes.setup: Everyone is moved by Jeff catching his enemy
jokes.punchline: Abed's cynical alternative explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed moment - brutally logical alternative to sentimental interpretation

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Or he just caught him because he's a sociopath and doesn't understand that you're supposed to let people win sometimes to make them feel good about themselves.

jokes.text: They kissed. You do have powers. I'm gonna die by werewolf
jokes.setup: Jeff and Annie kiss after winning debate
jokes.punchline: Shirley's acceptance that Abed is psychic and her werewolf fate
jokes.explanation: Good callback tying together multiple prediction threads with Shirley's dramatic conclusion

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: I always knew Abed was psychic. And I guess this means I'm a werewolf now.

jokes.text: In my next film, we discover that Pierce is an actual genius / Well, that's not gonna happen / No, it's not
jokes.setup: Abed mentions his next film idea about Pierce being a genius
jokes.punchline: Pierce's immediate rejection and Abed's matter-of-fact agreement
jokes.explanation: Great three-beat joke with Pierce's self-awareness and Abed's honest assessment

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: My next film is going to be about Pierce being a genius.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Yeah, okay.

jokes.text: I can't smoke a cigarette without thinking of a three-way in your hot tub. You did it
jokes.setup: Britta realizes Pierce's hypnosis worked
jokes.punchline: The disgusting association Pierce planted is preventing her from smoking
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the hypnosis subplot showing it worked despite the chaos

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I can't smoke anymore. Every time I try, I think of... Pierce's... and his...

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: My hypnosis actually worked?

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Unfortunately. You made me associate smoking with the image of you in a thong.

jokes.text: Just pat me
jokes.setup: Jeff and Annie have romantic tension after kissing
jokes.punchline: Jeff's awkward request for minimal physical contact
jokes.explanation: Good Jeff character moment showing his awkwardness with genuine emotion

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: So, about what happened... maybe we could just, like, not touch for a while?

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Jeff, we just kissed.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I know. That's why I'm asking.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: You want to avoid touching me?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Not avoid. Just... establish a no-contact policy. For both our sakes.

jokes.text: donde esta la biblioteca? me llamo t-bone, la arana discoteca
jokes.setup: Troy rapping in Spanish
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical Spanish mixing basic phrases with 'T-bone the disco spider'
jokes.explanation: Classic Community absurdist humor - became iconic meme. Perfect Troy randomness.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Yo, uh, hola, me llamo es Troy. Tengo un gato. El gato es muy malo. Mi pantalones son azules. Uh, T-bone the disco spider, uh, muy bien, muy bien.

tmdb_overview: As Jeff is drafted to help lead Greendale's debate team against the formidable City College, Abed's film-class projects appear to predict the future.
insight: Debate structure sustains 71 jokes across 45 minutes—format itself becomes the comedy engine.
